title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: June 02, 2026: Market Pauses After Record Run Amid AI Spending Scrutiny and Geopolitical Tensions

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Retreat from Record Highs; AI Spending and Geopolitical Concerns Weigh
items.summary: U.S. stock markets experienced a pullback on Tuesday, retreating from the record highs achieved on Monday, as investors grappled with fresh concerns over U.S.-Iran tensions and renewed scrutiny surrounding the substantial investments in artificial intelligence. After a strong close to the previous session, early Tuesday trading saw major indices dip, with a mixed sentiment prevailing throughout the day.

items.statContext: Down 0.21% on Tuesday. Closed Monday at 7,599.96, up 0.3%.
Down 0.1% on Tuesday. Closed Monday at 27,086.81, up 0.4%.
Down 0.41% on Tuesday. Closed Monday at 51,078.88, up 0.1%.

items.headline: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Soars on Strong Earnings and AI Demand
items.summary: Shares of Hewlett Packard Enterprise ($HPE) jumped significantly, with reports indicating gains between 26% and 37%, after the company announced a strong fiscal second-quarter earnings beat and substantially raised its full-year guidance, citing robust demand for its AI infrastructure.

items.headline: Marvell Technology Rallies on New AI Chip and Nvidia CEO's Endorsement
items.summary: Marvell Technology ($MRVL) saw its stock surge by as much as 25% following the unveiling of its Teralynx T100, an AI-optimized switch silicon, and an endorsement from Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, who called Marvell the 'next trillion-dollar company.'

items.headline: Victoria's Secret Jumps After Earnings Beat
items.summary: Victoria's Secret ($VSCO) shares surged by 45% after the company reported an earnings beat and lifted its outlook.

items.headline: Legend Biotech ($LEGN) Climbs on Positive CAR-T Therapy Data
items.summary: Legend Biotech Corp. ($LEGN) was up 26.1% at $32.17 after presenting data at the American Society of Clinical Oncology annual meeting showing early success in extending CAR-T therapy to solid tumors.

items.headline: Generac Holdings Rises on Data Center Agreement
items.summary: Generac Holdings ($GNRC) gained 9% after announcing an agreement to supply backup power generators for a hyperscale operator's data center network.

items.headline: Alphabet Slides on $80 Billion Stock Sale Plan for AI
items.summary: Alphabet ($GOOG / $GOOGL) shares fell sharply on Tuesday after the Google parent company announced plans to raise $80 billion through stock sales to fund its growing investments in artificial intelligence, sparking investor questions about the profitability justification for such massive spending.

items.headline: Above Food Ingredients Plummets on NASDAQ Determination Letter
items.summary: Above Food Ingredients Inc. ($ABVE) saw a drastic decline of 80.57% after receiving a NASDAQ determination letter under listing rules.

items.headline: NextCure, Inc. Drops Despite Positive Phase 1 Data
items.summary: NextCure, Inc. ($NXTC) fell 45.48% even as it presented positive SIM0505 Phase 1 dose escalation data in patients with gynecologic cancers at ASCO 2026.

items.headline: Technology and Energy Led Monday's Gains; Healthcare and Financials Lag Year-to-Date
items.summary: On Monday, technology and energy sectors were the top performers, with the Information Technology Select Sector SPDR (XLK) gaining 2.5% and the Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) rising 1.8%. However, for the year, healthcare, financial, and utilities sectors have notably lagged the broader market, presenting potential value opportunities. Nine out of eleven S&P 500 sectors ended in negative territory on Monday, indicating a narrow market rally.

items.headline: April 2026 Job Openings Rise to 7.6 Million, Hires and Separations Decline
items.summary: The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported today that the number of job openings increased to 7.6 million in April 2026. Over the month, hires and total separations decreased to 5.1 million and 5.0 million, respectively. Job openings saw a significant increase in professional and business services, while decreasing in finance and insurance.

items.headline: CEO Confidence Turns Negative in Q2 2026, Citing Worsening Economic Outlook
items.summary: The Conference Board Measure of CEO Confidence fell to 47 in the second quarter of 2026, down from 59 in Q1, indicating that negative outlooks now outnumber positive ones. A significant 40% of CEOs expect economic conditions to worsen over the next six months, reflecting a sharp shift from optimism to pessimism.

items.headline: Cleveland Fed President Hints at Potential Rate Hikes if Inflation Persists
items.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack suggested that policymakers might consider additional interest rate hikes if inflation remains elevated. The upcoming FOMC meeting on June 17, 2026, will be crucial, with expectations for no immediate rate change but potential for a hawkish revision to the Fed’s future rate outlook if inflation remains 'sticky' above the 2% target.

items.headline: Oil Prices Retreat Amid Shifting U.S.-Iran Peace Talk Signals
items.summary: Oil prices retreated on Tuesday after Monday's sharp surge, as uncertainty surrounding U.S.-Iran negotiations and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continued to influence markets. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude lost $1.26 to $90.90 a barrel, and Brent crude slipped $1.26 to $93.72 a barrel.

items.headline: Upcoming SpaceX IPO Marks Potential AI Sentiment Inflection Point
items.summary: Global investors are closely watching the upcoming SpaceX IPO, viewing it as a potential inflection point for AI sentiment. The offering is based on unprecedented valuation projections, and its performance could significantly influence broader perceptions of the AI theme.
